pg24-p1-s2:
At a certain point in the drinking of every alcoholic, he passes into a state where the most powerful desire to
stop drinking is of absolutely no avail.
pg24-p1-s3:
This tragic situation has already arrived in practically every case long before
it is suspected.
pg24-p1:
control. At a certain point in the drinking of every alcoholic, he passes into a state where the most powerful desire to
stop drinking is of absolutely no avail. This tragic situation has already arrived in practically every case long before
it is suspected.
pg24-p3-s1: The fact is that most alcoholics, for reasons yet obscure, have lost the power of choice in drink.
pg24-p3-s2:
Our so-called will
power becomes practically nonexistent.
pg24-p3-s3:
We are unable, at certain times, to bring into our consciousness with sufficient
force the memory of the suffering and humiliation of even a week or a month ago.
pg24-p3-s4:
We are without defense against the
first drink.
pg24-p3:
The fact is that most alcoholics, for reasons yet obscure, have lost the power of choice in drink. Our so-called will
power becomes practically nonexistent. We are unable, at certain times, to bring into our consciousness with sufficient
force the memory of the suffering and humiliation of even a week or a month ago. We are without defense against the
first drink.
pg24-p5-s1: The almost certain consequences that follow taking even a glass of beer do not crowd into the mind to deter us.
pg24-p5-s2:
If these
thoughts occur, they are hazy and readily supplanted with the old threadbare idea that this time we shall handle
ourselves like other people.
pg24-p5-s3:
There is a complete failure of the kind of defense that keeps one from putting his hand on
a hot stove.
pg24-p5:
The almost certain consequences that follow taking even a glass of beer do not crowd into the mind to deter us. If these
thoughts occur, they are hazy and readily supplanted with the old threadbare idea that this time we shall handle
ourselves like other people. There is a complete failure of the kind of defense that keeps one from putting his hand on
a hot stove.
pg24-p6-s1: The alcoholic may say to himself in the most casual way, "It won't burn me this time, so here's how!"
pg24-p6-s2:
Or perhaps he
doesn't think at all.
pg24-p6-s3:
How often have some of us begun to drink in this nonchalant way, and after the third or fourth,
pounded on the bar and said to ourselves, "For God's sake, how did I ever get started again?"
pg24-p6-s4:
Only to have that thought
supplanted by "Well, I'll stop with the sixth drink."
pg24-p6:
The alcoholic may say to himself in the most casual way, "It won't burn me this time, so here's how!" Or perhaps he
doesn't think at all. How often have some of us begun to drink in this nonchalant way, and after the third or fourth,
pounded on the bar and said to ourselves, "For God's sake, how did I ever get started again?" Only to have that thought
supplanted by "Well, I'll stop with the sixth drink." Or "What's the use anyhow?"
pg24-p7-s1:
When this sort of thinking is fully established in an individual with alcoholic tendencies, he has probably placed
himself beyond human aid, and unless locked up, may die or go permanently insane.
pg24-p7-s2:
These stark and ugly facts have been
confirmed by legions of alcoholics
pg24-p7:
When this sort of thinking is fully established in an individual with alcoholic tendencies, he has probably placed
himself beyond human aid, and unless locked up, may die or go permanently insane. These stark and ugly facts have been
confirmed by legions of alcoholics
